zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Seward於1793年2月13日出生在美國紐約州奧瑟格時，她的父親Noadiah Seward50歲，母親Sarah Swain46歲。她於1811年3月3日與Calvin Day結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1838年3月16日享年45歲，埋葬在美國紐約州奧瑟格。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Seward于1793年2月13日出生在美国纽约州奧瑟格时，她的父亲Noadiah Seward50岁，母亲Sarah Swain46岁。她于1811年3月3日与Calvin Day结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1838年3月16日享年45岁，埋葬在美国纽约州奧瑟格。

nameMeanings.name: Seward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Seward (Old English Sǣweard, from sǣ ‘sea’ + weard ‘guard’).
English: from the Middle English personal name Siward, Seward (Old English Sigeweard, from sige ‘victory’ + weard ‘guard’, or the equivalent Old Norse Sigwarth).
Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Suaird, Ó Suairt, usually Anglicized as Sword .

variants.name: Sewer
meanings: African American: probably an altered form of English   Seward  .
Swiss German: habitational name for someone from either of two places called Seewen (Schwyz and Solothurn).
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian   Sever  .

variants.name: Sword
meanings: English: probably a shortened form of   Seward  .
English: nickname from Middle English sword(e), swerd(e) ‘sword’, perhaps used for a swordsman or armourer.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Suaird, Ó Suairt ‘descendant of Suart’, a personal name derived from Old Norse Sigvarthr ‘victory keeper’, a cognate of Old English Sigeweard; compare Seward 2.

variants.name: Sheward
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Seward  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.title: Creating the Eleventh Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eleventh Amendment restricts the ability of any people to start a lawsuit against the states in federal court.

historicEvents.title: Albany is Named Capital of New York
historicEvents.description: Albany became the capital of New York in 1797. Albany is the oldest continuous settlement of the original 13 colonies.
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

historicEvents.title: Panic! of 1819
historicEvents.description: With the Aftermath of the Napoleonic Wars the global market for trade was down. During this time, America had its first financial crisis and it lasted for only two years.

historicEvents.title: Slavery Becomes Illegal in New York State
historicEvents.description: During the years 1799 to 1827, New York went through a period of gradual emancipation. A Gradual Emancipation Law was passed in 1799 which freed slave children born after July 4, 1799. However, they were indentured until 25 years old for women and 28 years old for men. A law passed 1817 which freed slaves born before 1799, yet delayed their emancipation for ten years. All remaining slaves were freed in New York State on July 4, 1827.

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Seward was born on 13 February 1793, in Otsego, New York, United States, her father, Noadiah Seward, was 50 and her mother, Sarah Swain, was 46. She married Calvin Day on 3 March 1811. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 16 March 1838, at the age of 45, and was buried in Otsego, New York, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Seward est née le 13 février 1793, à Otsego, New York, États-Unis, son père, Noadiah Seward, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Sarah Swain, avait 46 ans. Elle a épousé Calvin Day le 3 mars 1811. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1838, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à Otsego, New York, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Sewardが1793年2月13日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴで生まれた時，父Noadiah Sewardは50歳，母Sarah Swainは46歳であった。1811年3月3日にCalvin Dayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1838年3月16日に45歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州オトセゴにて埋葬された。

ko.Granville, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 그랜빌
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Seward(이)가 1793년 2월 13일 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Noadiah Seward(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Swain(은)는 46세였다. 그녀는 1811년 3월 3일 Calvin Day(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1838년 3월 16일 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 뉴욕 주 츠고에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Seward родилась 13 февраля 1793 г. в местности оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Noadiah Seward было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Swain, 46. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Calvin Day 3 марта 1811 г. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 16 марта 1838 г. в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности оцего, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты.
